title: Elektrik ve Elektronik Mühendisliği (İngilizce) Bölümü - Bölüm Hakkında
content:
‑

Akademik Kadro

Kariyer Olanakları

Öğrenci

Araştırma Projeleri:

Yayınlar:

Electrical and Electronics Engineering (English) is an interdisciplinary field covering the generation, distribution, and applications of electrical energy, as well as the design and control of electronic systems. Students are equipped with a comprehensive curriculum, including courses on electrical circuits, microprocessors, communication systems, and energy management. Our program aims to train innovative, research-oriented engineers with international-level competence.

History & Academic Structure

The program began as a 100% English curriculum in the 2014–2015 academic year and has been continued since 2015–2016 with 30% English instruction.

The medium of instruction is English, and the curriculum aligns with European Credit Transfer and Accumulation System (ECTS) standards.

Erasmus and international internship/exchange opportunities are available.

Academic Staff

Our faculty consists of experts with international experience. An up-to-date list is available on our website.

Labs & Practical Facilities

Hands-on training is provided in laboratories for electrical circuits, basic electronics, and microprocessors.

Theoretical knowledge is reinforced through laboratory and project-based work.

Internships & Graduation Requirements

A total of 40 working days of mandatory internship (20 days in the summer of 2nd year, 20 days in the summer of 3rd year).

To graduate, students must complete at least 240 ECTS, pass all courses with at least a DD grade, and maintain a minimum GPA of 2.00/4.00.

Career Opportunities

Our graduates may pursue careers in system design, production, maintenance, energy systems, automation, and communications within the private sector, public organizations, R&D, consulting, or academia.

International Programs & Scholarships

Exchange programs such as Erasmus and Farabi are offered.

There are opportunities to study with full scholarships or at a 50% tuition discount.

Projects, Publications & Patents

• Student Research Projects:

In 2022, student Sevda Açıkça was granted support under the TÜBİTAK 2209‑A University Student Research Projects Program for her project titled “Multipurpose 3‑Phase Brushless Motor Driver and Electronic Board Design.”

Also in 2022, students Onur Gönüllü and Şeref Can Seyrek received support under the TÜBİTAK 2209‑B program for their project titled “Next‑Generation Single‑Switch Quasi Resonance Scalable Induction Cooker System.”

Publications:

Selected international publications from our faculty include:

“Distribution Transformer Winding Fault Detection Based on Hybrid Wavelet‑CNN” (WILEY International Transactions on Electrical Energy Systems, 2025) by Assoc. Prof. Dr. Ataberk Necati.

“Decentralized Robust Power System Stabilization Using Ellipsoid‑Based Sliding Mode Control” (MDPI Energies, 2024) by Dr. Farag Ali El‑sheikhi.

“Electronically Charge‑Controlled Tunable Meminductor Emulator Circuit with OTAs and its Applications” (IEEE Access, 2023) by Dr. Durmuş Ersoy.

“Quasi Resonant Inverter Load Recognition Method” (IEEE Access, 2023) by Dr. Metin Öztürk, Dr. Fatih Züngör, and Lecturer Barış Öz.
